questions.question: Define work in physics.
questions.answer: Work is said to be done when a force is applied on an object and it is displaced in the direction of the force.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of work?
questions.answer: The SI unit of work is Joule (J).

questions.question: State the formula for work done.
questions.answer: Work done, W = Force × Displacement × cosθ.

questions.question: What is the condition for zero work?
questions.answer: Work is zero if the displacement is perpendicular to the applied force or if there is no displacement.

questions.question: Define kinetic energy.
questions.answer: Kinetic energy is the energy possessed by a body due to its motion.

questions.question: State the formula for kinetic energy.
questions.answer: Kinetic energy, KE = 1/2 × mass × velocity² = 1/2 mv².

questions.question: Define potential energy.
questions.answer: Potential energy is the energy possessed by a body due to its position or configuration.

questions.question: State the formula for potential energy of a body near the earth's surface.
questions.answer: Potential energy, PE = mass × gravity × height = mgh.

questions.question: What is the unit of energy?
questions.answer: The SI unit of energy is Joule (J).

questions.question: Define power.
questions.answer: Power is the rate at which work is done.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power?
questions.answer: The SI unit of power is Watt (W).

questions.question: State the formula for power.
questions.answer: Power = Work / Time.

questions.question: Define work-energy theorem.
questions.answer: The work done on a body is equal to the change in its kinetic energy.

questions.question: What is mechanical energy?
questions.answer: Mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic energy and potential energy of a body.

questions.question: State the law of conservation of energy.
questions.answer: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed; it can only be transformed from one form to another.

questions.question: What is meant by positive work?
questions.answer: When the force applied on a body and the displacement are in the same direction, the work done is positive.

questions.question: What is meant by negative work?
questions.answer: When the force applied on a body and the displacement are in opposite directions, the work done is negative.

questions.question: Give an example of conservative force.
questions.answer: Gravity is an example of a conservative force.

questions.question: Give an example of non-conservative force.
questions.answer: Friction is an example of a non-conservative force.

questions.question: Define work done by a constant force.
questions.answer: Work done by a constant force = magnitude of force × displacement × cosθ, where θ is the angle between force and displacement.

questions.question: A body is at rest. What is its kinetic energy?
questions.answer: If a body is at rest, its kinetic energy is zero.

questions.question: When does a body have maximum potential energy near the earth's surface?
questions.answer: A body has maximum potential energy at its maximum height from the ground.

questions.question: What is the relation between work and energy?
questions.answer: Work done on a body results in a change in its energy.

questions.question: Define instantaneous power.
questions.answer: Instantaneous power is the rate at which work is done at a particular instant of time.

questions.question: State the relation between work and force when displacement is along the force.
questions.answer: If displacement is along the force, work done = force × displacement.
